Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Let me think about the activities and their relationships for an order fulfillment process Activities RO Receive Order CA Check Availability RI Reserve Items PI Pick Items QC Quality Check PO Pack Order GSL Generate Shipping Label D Dispatch IC Invoice Customer RP Record Payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Equivalence If the first activity occurs then it has the same occurrences as the second one RO and CA both happen exactly once per order If an order is received availability is checked and vice versa

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Actually let me think more carefully In a simple order fulfillment process all the physical fulfillment steps CA RI PI QC PO GSL D each happen exactly once if they happen And IC and RP each happen at most once

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: So equivalence pairs RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D IC RP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always Before If the first activity occurs then the second activity should have been executed previously RO before CA CA before RI RI before PI PI before QC QC before PO PO before GSL GSL before D IC before RP

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Could also include non adjacent pairs like RO before PI RO before D Let me list direct precedence Also CA before any later step RI before any later step and so on

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For a proper order fulfillment RO must happen first if the order is processed Then CA RI PI QC PO GSL D in that order Then IC then RP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always Before if first occurs second has occurred before RO before CA RO before RI RO before PI RO before QC RO before PO RO before GSL RO before D CA before RI CA before PI CA before QC CA before PO CA before GSL CA before D RI before PI RI before QC RI before PO RI before GSL RI before D PI before QC PI before PO PI before GSL PI before D QC before PO QC before GSL QC before D PO before GSL PO before D GSL before D IC before RP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always After if first occurs second occurs after CA after RO RI after RO and RI after CA PI after RO and PI after CA and PI after RI QC after RO and QC after CA and QC after RI and QC after PI PO after RO and PO after CA and PO after RI and PO after PI and PO after QC GSL after RO and GSL after CA and GSL after RI and GSL after PI and GSL after QC and GSL after PO D after RO and D after CA and D after RI and D after PI and D after QC and D after PO and D after GSL RP after IC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Never Together In a normal process all activities would coexist There might not be any never together pairs In this scenario there do not seem to be mutually exclusive activities described

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Activity Occurrences Bounds on number of occurrences for each activity RO 0 1 CA 0 1 RI 0 1 PI 0 1 QC 0 1 PO 0 1 GSL 0 1 D 0 1 IC 0 1 RP 0 1

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Directly Follows Constraints After RO comes CA After CA comes RI After RI comes PI After PI comes QC After QC comes PO After PO comes GSL After GSL comes D After IC comes RP

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: After D comes IC invoice after dispatch or IC could come before D Hmm

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Actually I need to be careful If I put D IC in directly follows then every occurrence of D must be immediately followed by IC That might be too restrictive Let me remove that from directly follows

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The IC and RP are financial activities that follow the logistics chain

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For IC and RP IC must happen before RP you invoice first then record payment So I have IC RP in equivalence IC RP in always before and RP IC in always after Good

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: One more thought should I consider D IC and IC RP in the directly follows That would mean after D comes IC and after IC comes RP This would create a linear chain RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP This makes a lot of sense for a complete order fulfillment process

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: In many distribution centers invoicing happens independently or even before dispatch proforma invoice But for a complete order fulfillment process it makes sense to invoice after dispatch

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Updated log skeleton equivalence RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP always before full chain always after full reverse chain never together empty activity frequencies all zero or one directly follows RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP
